reasoning: Variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (vCJD) is a prion disease for which no vaccine currently exists. Prion diseases are notoriously difficult to vaccinate against because they involve the misfolding of normal host proteins, making immune targeting highly complex without inducing autoimmunity. There are currently no advanced clinical trials for a vCJD vaccine that could plausibly finish and gain recognition in the next six months. The base rate of a completely novel vaccine appearing and being recognized on Wikipedia in a timeframe of 1 to 6 months for a condition that has eluded vaccination for decades is negligible. The only real route to a YES resolution would be Wikipedia vandalism or a misunderstanding of an early-stage animal trial by an editor. Therefore, my probabilities remain at the baseline lower bounds, increasing slightly over time to account for growing but still extremely small uncertainty.
